返回本批次

电话旅行

Phone Trips

www.wideweb.com 作者 bookofjoe 分数 69 评论 6 2026/04/12 00:43
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 各位网络旅行者，大家好，我的电话飞客账号是 Mark Bernay，35 年前我曾经进行过电话旅行。是的，这是真的：就像上面图片中的人一样，我会开车去小镇主要是为了玩公用电话。我经常带着我值得信赖的 Craig 212 便携式 3 英寸卷轴录音机（这是在盒式磁带流行之前）来记录电话噪音并为我的朋友讲述有关它们的信息。我不再用电话旅行了，你可能会认为这是因为我长大了，但不，我从来没有这样做过。我不再被电话绊倒的原因是现在全国所有的电话都差不多，而且真的很无聊。
  2. 这张照片显示了我 1968 年左右的录音设备，我用它来编辑这些磁带并准备在公共电话号码上播放。我现在的办公桌也同样凌乱，但用的是个人电脑，而不是卷盘式录音机。
  3. 当我十几岁的时候在洛杉矶长大时，我第一次开始玩手机——不是用手机说话，而是玩交换系统和网络。我的家人从太平洋电话公司（现在的太平洋贝尔公司）领土搬到了通用电话公司。我注意到拨号音响起时、拨打号码时的数字之间、拨号后和呼叫转接等方面电话发出的噪音有很大差异。我阅读了大量有关电话交换系统的文章，并参观了许多电话公司的交换机室以了解发生了什么情况。
  4. 1968 年，我从洛杉矶搬到了西雅图。西雅图本身的电话交换系统与洛杉矶的太平洋电话公司或通用电话公司完全不同。当我开始在西雅图地区开车时，我注意到许多小镇都有自己独立的电话公司，每个公司都有不同的设备。我在洛杉矶（后来在西雅图）的电话飞客朋友想听听我告诉他们的各种电话噪音，因此我将录音机的麦克风放在公用电话的耳机上进行录音。 （我知道电话拾音线圈，但它们从公用电话的荧光灯中拾取了太多嗡嗡声。）
  5. 不幸的是，制作这些磁带时我并不喜欢摄影，而且我也没有自己在电话旅行中的照片。我从一位同样进行电话旅行的朋友那里得到了顶部的照片，但照片中的人都没有参与这些录音。投币电话的那位先生很有名，您可能听说过他。

Original Summary

  1. Greetings fellow web trippers, my phone phreak handle is Mark Bernay and 35 years ago I used to go on phone trips. Yes, it's true: just like the people in the picture at the top, I would drive around to small towns primarily for the purpose of playing with their payphones. I often brought along my trusty Craig 212 portable 3-inch reel-to-reel tape recorder (this was before cassettes were popular) to record the phone noises and narrate information about them for my friends. I don't go on phone trips anymore and you are probably thinking that this is because I grew up, but no, I never did. The reason I stopped phone tripping is that all phones are about the same all over the country nowadays and they are really boring.
  2. This picture shows my recording equipment around 1968, which I used to edit these tapes and prepare them for playing on a public phone number. My current desk is just as messy, but with PC's instead of reel-to-reel tape recorders.
  3. I first started playing with phones -- not talking on them, but playing with the switching systems and the network -- when I was a teenager growing up in Los Angeles. My family moved from Pacific Telephone (now Pacific Bell) territory to General Telephone. I noticed big differences in the noises the phone made as the dial tone came on, in between digits as numbers were dialed, after dialing and the call was switching through, etc. I did a lot of reading about telephone switching systems and visited many phone company switchrooms to learn what was going on.
  4. I moved from Los Angeles to Seattle in 1968. Seattle itself had completely different telephone switching systems than either Pacific Telephone or General Telephone in Los Angeles. When I started driving around the Seattle area I noticed that many of the little towns had their own independent phone companies and every one had different equipment. My phone phreak friends in Los Angeles (and later in Seattle) wanted to hear this variety of phone noises I was telling them about, so I tape recorded by holding the microphone of the recorder against the earphones of the payphones. (I was aware of telephone pickup coils, but they picked up too much hum from the fluorescent lights in payphones.)
  5. Unfortunately I wasn't into photography at the time these tapes were made and I have no pictures of myself on a phone trip. I got the photo at the top from a friend who also went on phone trips, but none of the people in that picture took part in these recordings. The gentleman at the payphone is famous and you probably heard of him.

评论（默认中文）

tehryanx 2026/04/12 02:22
我在 2000 年代初第一次镜像这些，因为我担心它最终会消失。我的镜子已经消失几十年了，原来的镜子还保存着。 :)
I first mirrored these in the early 2000s because I was worried it would eventually vanish. my mirror has been gone for decades, and the original survives. :)

plapsley 2026/04/12 02:55
这是一个很棒的网站。 埃文·门贝尔 (Evan Doorbell) 的《我如何成为电话飞客》或《远距离之声》中的任何一首都特别棒。
This is such a great site. Any of Evan Doorbell's "How I Became a Phone Phreak" or "Sounds of Long Distance" are especially great.

bookofjoe 2026/04/12 01:58
>好吧，那么这些录音都是关于什么的呢？他们为什么在这里？

>大家好，各位网络旅行者，我的电话飞客账号是 Mark Bernay，35 年前我曾经进行过电话旅行。是的，这是真的：就像上面图片中的人一样，我会开车去小镇主要是为了玩公用电话。我经常带着我值得信赖的 Craig 212 便携式 3 英寸卷轴录音机（这是在盒式磁带流行之前）来记录电话噪音并为我的朋友讲述有关它们的信息。我不再用电话旅行了，你可能会认为这是因为我长大了，但不，我从来没有这样做过。我不再被电话绊倒的原因是现在全国所有的电话都差不多，而且真的很无聊。

>这张照片显示了我1968年左右的录音设备，我用它来编辑这些磁带并准备在公共电话号码上播放。我现在的办公桌也同样凌乱，但用的是个人电脑，而不是卷盘式录音机。

>该页面已有 1237108 次访问。

……………………

>小蓝盒子的秘密 (1971)

https://www.ckts.info/downloads/articles/Esquire%20Magazine%...

>OK, so what are these recordings all about? Why are they here?

>Greetings fellow web trippers, my phone phreak handle is Mark Bernay and 35 years ago I used to go on phone trips. Yes, it's true: just like the people in the picture at the top, I would drive around to small towns primarily for the purpose of playing with their payphones. I often brought along my trusty Craig 212 portable 3-inch reel-to-reel tape recorder (this was before cassettes were popular) to record the phone noises and narrate information about them for my friends. I don't go on phone trips anymore and you are probably thinking that this is because I grew up, but no, I never did. The reason I stopped phone tripping is that all phones are about the same all over the country nowadays and they are really boring.

>This picture shows my recording equipment around 1968, which I used to edit these tapes and prepare them for playing on a public phone number. My current desk is just as messy, but with PC's instead of reel-to-reel tape recorders.

>There have been 1237108 accesses to this page.

.........................

>Secrets of the Little Blue Box (1971)

https://www.ckts.info/downloads/articles/Esquire%20Magazine%...

很棒的网站！但有些外部链接已失效（404）：

> Phone Tripper Frank Wilsey 参观了华盛顿州西雅图的老式电话设备博物馆和缅因州北埃尔斯沃思的新英格兰电话博物馆。

Great site! Some external links are dead (404) though:

> Phone Tripper Frank Wilsey visited the Vintage Telephone Equipment Museum in Seattle, WA and the New England Museum of Telephony in North Ellsworth, Maine.

ChrisArchitect 2026/04/12 05:29
在某种程度上，这部新纪录片讲述了盲人电话飞客的迷人生活 欢乐泡泡。

https://www.joybubblesthemovie.com/

https://www.inverse.com/entertainment/joybubbles-documentary...

Related in some ways, the new documentary on the fascinating life of blind phone phreak Joybubbles.
